calendar.matters:
Petition To Compel An Accounting And For The Removal Of Cotrustee Charlotte Brewster And For Appointment Of Successor
Off Calendar Feb-27-2014 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mary Wiederhold for and with Cynthia Carter.

Ms. Wiederhold requested a continuance to allow her Client the opportunity to consult with a landlord attorney to deal with the issue of the Niece vacating the premises and to address the issue of the Brother moving downstairs. Ms. Wiederhold also spoke of the Co-Trustees performance when knowing that the Court is watching over the trust. The Court will issue an order indicating what the next step will be.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)

Petition To Compel An Accounting And For The Removal Of Cotrustee Charlotte Brewster And For Appointment Of Successor
Off Calendar Jan-15-2014 Continued To Feb-26-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearance: Robert Smith specially appearing for Cynthia Carter (Petitioner).

The Court noted that this matter has been continued 14 times. Furthermore, at the last hearing, the Court has indicated that if no action is taken, this petition will go off calendar. Mr. Smith reported that they have resolved but one issue and that he requested for one more continuance. The Petition to Compel an Accounting and for Removal, etc. was continued to February 26, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

Petition To Compel An Accounting And For The Removal Of Cotrustee Charlotte Brewster And For Appointment Of Successor
Off Calendar Dec-18-2013 Continued To Jan-15-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mary Wiederhold for Cynthia Carter (Petitioner).

Ms. Wiederhold stated that the issue of removal of a family member and subsequent rental of a trust property has not been accomplished, that the Co-Trustees are not in agreement as to handling of the issue, that she spoke with her Client about a month ago and that she will go ahead with the petition if they can not reach resolution. The Court stated that this matter has been before it too long without any movement, that nothing has been filed or done since 2011, and that it is inclined to take the matter off calendar. Ms. Wiederhold expressed concern about her Client's financial status and having to pay additional filing fees. The Petition to Compel an Accounting and for Removal of Co-Trustee, etc. was continued to January 15, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed Ms. Wiederhold that if nothing happens by then, that the matter will go off calendar.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Clark. (Not Reported)

Petition To Compel An Accounting And For The Removal Of Cotrustee Charlotte Brewster And For Appointment Of Successor

Petition To Compel An Accounting And For The Removal Of Cotrustee Charlotte Brewster And For Appointment Of Successor
Off Calendar Oct-26-2011 Continued To Dec-07-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Compel An Accounting And For The Removal Of Cotrustee Charlotte Brewster And For Appointment Of Successor
Off Calendar Sep-07-2011 Continued To Oct-26-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Sellards for Charlotte Brewster.

The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than October 20, 2011. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by October 21, 2011. The matter was continued to and the Parties are to return on October 26, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Yee.

Petition To Compel An Accounting And For The Removal Of Cotrustee Charlotte Brewster And For Appointment Of Successor
Off Calendar Aug-10-2011 Continued To Sep-07-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Compel An Accounting And For The Removal Of Cotrustee Charlotte Brewster And For Appointment Of Successor
Off Calendar Jun-15-2011 Continued To Aug-10-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mary Wiederhold for and with Cynthia Carter (Petitioner). Robert Sellards for and with Charlotte Brewster.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the removal of Ms. Brewster as Co-Trustee, documents not being produced, the accountings, expenditures, rents, distribution, adjustments to the distributions, past rents and past income, the loan to Mr. Brewster, building up a reserve, etc. The Court would like to see the Sisters exchange information so that they can evaluate where you stand, to see them talk about whether rent is going to be paid by the Brother or by Cynthia, whether Charlotte should be able to take out some amount of money since others appear to be living rent free to compensate for that and to exchange information about what's gone on in the past in terms of bills. The matter was continued to August 10, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of resolution of the above.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Rob Harris.

Petition To Compel An Accounting And For The Removal Of Cotrustee Charlotte Brewster And For Appointment Of Successor
Off Calendar May-16-2011 Continued To Jun-15-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Ex Parte Application For The Removal Of Co-Trustee Charlotte Brewster (Probate Code Section 15642(B)), Or In The Alternative, Temporary Removal Of Co-Trustee Charlotte Brewster'S Trust Powers Pending A Noticed Motion; Declaration Of Petitioner Cynthia D. Carter
Off Calendar Mar-22-2011 Granted Per Written Order
